text.title: Prompt Portability Audit Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules + 2 terminal conclusions every portability audit report must enforce.

rule.statement: Scope (artefact set) is frozen at audit start. Adding files mid-audit rejected — start a new audit instead.
rule.rationale: Scope drift invalidates findings; reviewers cannot tell what was actually covered.

rule.statement: Every finding cites a criterion (rule_id or policy line). Findings without criterion are demoted to hypotheses.
Every finding carries severity (low|medium|high) + named owner + deadline (ISO date).
Every finding ships a reproducible evidence anchor (query / file path / git sha).
rule.rationale: Non-reproducible evidence cannot be verified at remediation.

rule.statement: Report ends with a follow-up backlog link (ticket queue / SDD folder).
rule.rationale: Without a backlog the report is shelfware.

rule.statement: Migration planned AND scope ≥1 prompt → run the 5-step procedure.
No migration planned AND no quarterly health check due → skip.
